title: NOC 75101 – Material handlers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 75101 professionals are the essential gears of Canada’s logistics and supply chain machine. As a TEER 5 occupation (formerly Skill Level C), Material Handlers move, load, and unload materials by hand or using specialized equipment in warehouses, manufacturing plants, and retail centers. In 2026, with the explosive growth of e-commerce and the "Just-in-Time" delivery model, these workers are more critical than ever to keeping the Canadian economy moving. For immigration, this role is a primary target for the New 2026 TR to PR Pathway and various Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level5Major Group75 – Helpers and labourers and other transport drivers, operators and labourersSub-Major Group751 – Helpers and labourersMinor Group7510 – Longshore workers and material handlersNOC Code75101

duties: Loading & Unloading: Manually move products and materials onto and off trucks, railway cars, and loading docks.
Equipment Operation: Operate industrial trucks, tractors, and loaders to transport materials to and from transportation vehicles and storage areas.
Inventory Support: Perform material handling activities such as counting, weighing, sorting, packing, and unpacking items.
Liquid/Bulk Handling: Connect hoses or pipes to load/unload petroleum, chemicals, or other liquid products into tank trucks or storage units.
Sorting & Packing: Open containers and crates, fill warehouse orders, and assist in taking inventory.
Documentation: Use handheld scanners (RF guns) to track inventory movements and update Warehouse Management Systems (WMS).
employment_requirements: Some secondary school education is usually required.
On-the-Job Training: Most training is provided by the employer, typically lasting from a few days to several weeks.

Certifications (Employer Specific):Forklift Certification is required for operators (e.g., Reach, Counterbalance, or Order Picker).WHMIS (Workplace Hazardous Materials Information System) is mandatory for handling chemicals.TDG (Transportation of Dangerous Goods) may be required for specific industrial roles.
Forklift Certification is required for operators (e.g., Reach, Counterbalance, or Order Picker).
WHMIS (Workplace Hazardous Materials Information System) is mandatory for handling chemicals.
TDG (Transportation of Dangerous Goods) may be required for specific industrial roles.
Physical strength and stamina are mandatory for manual handlers who frequently lift heavy materials (up to 50 lbs).
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, specialized material handlers in high-tech sectors like Pharmaceuticals or Automotive Parts often command a 10–15% premium over the general warehouse wage.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate that despite increasing warehouse automation, the volume of goods moving through the Canadian supply chain will outpace the rate of robotic replacement. According to the Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS), "Replacement Demand" due to retirements and internal promotions will create over 35,000 job openings across Canada through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The demand for Material Handlers in 2026 is Strong and Stable. The massive expansion of distribution centers in Ontario (Peel Region/Halton) and British Columbia (Lower Mainland) has created thousands of year-round positions. While automation (AMRs and cobots) is becoming common in large hubs, the need for human handlers to manage complex sorting, quality checks, and non-standardized goods remains high. In 2026, industry reports show a 9% increase in demand for handlers with specialized "Cold Chain" or "Hazardous Goods" experience.
E-commerce Hub Expansion: Work in some of the most technologically advanced fulfillment centers in North America.
Rapid Employment Entry: This is one of the fastest sectors for newcomers to enter the Canadian workforce, often requiring minimal formal education to start.
Pathways to Specialization: Many material handlers transition into high-paying roles as Forklift Operators, Inventory Leads, or Warehouse Supervisors.
Strategic PR Pathways: This TEER 5 role is a core beneficiary of the New 2026 TR to PR Pathway, which prioritizes essential transport and supply chain workers.
pathways: New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A dedicated stream for 33,000 workers already in Canada, specifically targeting "Supply Chain and Material Handling" roles.
OINP Employer Job Offer (In-Demand Skills): Frequently targets material handlers for regions outside the GTA.
BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled (ELSS): A direct route for workers currently employed in British Columbia’s warehousing sector.
Saskatchewan SINP (Hard-to-Fill Skills): A high-priority stream for material handlers in the province's manufacturing and retail sectors.
